nameMeanings.name: Whitaker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from Whitacre in Warwickshire or High Whitaker in Lancashire, both of whose names derive from Old English hwīt ‘white’ + æcer ‘field’. The name may also be derived from Wheatacre in Norfolk or Whiteacre in Waltham (Kent), both of which derive from Old English hwǣte ‘wheat’ + æcer ‘field’. Compare Whittaker .

nameMeanings.name: Lavenia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Name, according to Roman mythology, of the wife of Aeneas, and thus the mother of the Roman people. Legend had it that she gave her name to the Latin town of Lavinium, but in fact she was almost certainly invented to explain the place name, which is of pre-Roman origin. She was said to be the daughter of King Latinus , who was similarly invented to account for the name of Latium. The name Lavinia is recorded in England from the 17th century onwards.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Lavenia Whitaker was born in 1430, in Farnley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with John Farnley. She died in 1461, in Leeds, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 31.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Lavenia Whitaker(은)는 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 패른리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John Farnley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1461년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 리드즈에서 31세의 나이로 사망했다.
